target.title: docs: add missing breakPreference field to blockStreamingChunk config example
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `breakPreference` field exists in `BlockStreamingChunkConfig` (types.base.ts line 34) but is missing from the docs example
- **Why it matters:** Users only see `minChars` and `maxChars` ŌĆö `breakPreference` is invisible despite being a valid config option
- **What changed:** Added `breakPreference: "paragraph"` with inline comment `// paragraph | newline | sentence` to the `blockStreamingChunk` example in `docs/gateway/config-agents.md` line 662
- **What did NOT change:** No source code, runtime behavior, or tests modified. Docs only.

## Change Type

- [x] Docs

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `breakPreference` exists in the type definition but was never added to the docs example
- Missing detection / guardrail: No automated check for type-to-docs coverage
- Contributing context: N/A

## Regression Test Plan

N/A ŌĆö docs-only change, no executable code modified.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None ŌĆö runtime behavior unchanged.

## Diagram

```text
Before: blockStreamingChunk: { minChars: 800, maxChars: 1200 }
After:  blockStreamingChunk: { minChars: 800, maxChars: 1200, breakPreference: "paragraph" }, // paragraph | newline | sentence
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets

```
> findstr /n "breakPreference" docs\gateway\config-agents.md
(no results before fix)

> findstr /n "breakPreference" src\config\types.base.ts
34:  breakPreference?: "paragraph" | "newline" | "sentence";
```

## Human Verification

- Verified: `breakPreference` present in types.base.ts line 34 and zod-schema.core.ts; absent from docs before this change; added style matches surrounding lines
- What I did **not** verify: Runtime behavior of `breakPreference` values (out of scope)

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

None ŌĆö docs-only change, no runtime impact.
target.author: ayesha-aziz123
